description:
You are an automation specialist who has been asked to prepare a tiny text-processing workflow that relies only on the classic GNU tools awk, sed and (optionally) grep/cut/sort.  
Everything must be done inside the directory  

/home/user/automation_logs  

A file called  

/home/user/automation_logs/system_report.txt  

is already there.  Your job is to create three new artefacts from that file and to write a very small plain-text log that documents what you did.

Input file (exact, seven lines):

2023-09-14 10:00:01 [INFO] System boot  
2023-09-14 10:01:15 [WARN] Disk space low  
2023-09-14 10:05:42 [ERROR] Failed to start service A  
2023-09-14 10:10:30 [INFO] Service B started  
2023-09-14 10:15:00 [WARN] High memory usage  
2023-09-14 10:20:20 [ERROR] Unhandled exception  
2023-09-14 10:25:55 [INFO] Health check passed  

Tasks to accomplish (use 5-10 one-liners built around awk and/or sed):

1. Extract only the lines that contain the tag “[ERROR]” (preserve the original order) and save them to  
   /home/user/automation_logs/errors_only.txt

2. Produce a cleaned version of the full report in which every literal “[WARN]” tag has been changed to “[WARNING]” (case sensitive) and every trailing blank or tab at the end of a line has been removed.  
   Save the result as  
   /home/user/automation_logs/cleaned_report.txt

3. Create a comma-separated summary called  
   /home/user/automation_logs/severity_summary.csv  
   The file must contain exactly four lines in this precise order:
      • header line: “Severity,Count”  
      • count for tag “[ERROR]”  
      • count for tag “[INFO]”  
      • count for tag “[WARNING]”  
   Example format (values will be checked by an automated test):
   Severity,Count  
   ERROR,2  
   INFO,3  
   WARNING,2  

4. Make a tiny plain-text log named  
   /home/user/automation_logs/process.log  
   It has to consist of exactly three numbered lines (no timestamps) that describe, in order, what was created.  
   Required content and format (each line starts with the digit, a dot, a space, then the text):
   1. errors_only.txt created  
   2. cleaned_report.txt created  
   3. severity_summary.csv created  

Rules & hints:
• Only standard command-line utilities that ship with a minimal GNU/Linux environment may be used; do NOT write your own scripts or C programs.  
• Do NOT move or delete the original system_report.txt.  
• The automated checker will fail if any extra spaces, blank lines, or different letter casing appear in the output files.  
• All four output files must be located directly inside /home/user/automation_logs and must be world-readable.  
• Keep the whole solution within 5-10 shell commands.  
• A good sequence would be something like “grep … > …”, “sed … > …”, “awk … > …”, etc.—but the precise commands are up to you.  

When you are done you may cat the created files to verify their contents, but that is optional.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent runs):
Directory /home/user/automation_logs exists and contains exactly one file named system_report.txt with the seven lines shown in the public task description (line endings are Unix LF).

Final state expected after correct completion:

1. /home/user/automation_logs/errors_only.txt  
   Content (two lines):
   2023-09-14 10:05:42 [ERROR] Failed to start service A
   2023-09-14 10:20:20 [ERROR] Unhandled exception

2. /home/user/automation_logs/cleaned_report.txt  
   Content (seven lines, “[WARN]” replaced, no trailing whitespace):
   2023-09-14 10:00:01 [INFO] System boot
   2023-09-14 10:01:15 [WARNING] Disk space low
   2023-09-14 10:05:42 [ERROR] Failed to start service A
   2023-09-14 10:10:30 [INFO] Service B started
   2023-09-14 10:15:00 [WARNING] High memory usage
   2023-09-14 10:20:20 [ERROR] Unhandled exception
   2023-09-14 10:25:55 [INFO] Health check passed

3. /home/user/automation_logs/severity_summary.csv  
   Content (four lines):
   Severity,Count
   ERROR,2
   INFO,3
   WARNING,2

4. /home/user/automation_logs/process.log  
   Content (three lines):
   1. errors_only.txt created
   2. cleaned_report.txt created
   3. severity_summary.csv created

Permissions:
• Existing directory /home/user/automation_logs is 755 (drwxr-xr-x).  
• system_report.txt starts as 644 (rw-r--r--).  
• The agent must ensure that the three new output files are created with mode 644 or more permissive; any chmod operation is optional but acceptable.

The automated test will:
a) Verify that /home/user/automation_logs/errors_only.txt, cleaned_report.txt, severity_summary.csv, and process.log exist.  
b) Confirm that their contents match exactly (byte-for-byte) the data given above.  
c) Check that system_report.txt was not modified.  
d) Optionally check that each file is world-readable.

No additional files other than the ones mentioned may appear in /home/user/automation_logs.
